meta_title: For Founders: project management to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Project Management to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop watching tutorials and start shipping. Use this founder-focused checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to master project management and monetize faster.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop consuming and start shipping. By treating your MVP as a managed project rather than a coding exercise, you create accountability. Use Antigravity to structure your architecture and Claude Code to automate execution, shifting your focus from syntax to sustainable, revenue-generating SaaS features.
content.intro:
The screen glows with another 'Build a To-Do App' video, but your bank account remains at zero. You are trapped in the high-definition loop of tutorial hell, convincing yourself that the next course will be the one that unlocks your vision. It won't. The difference between a consumer of content and a founder of a product is the ruthless application of project management to your workflow.

Real software isn't built in a sandbox; it's forged through the friction of scope, deadlines, and market validation. It is time to stop being a student and start acting like a CEO. With tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can bypass the manual drudgery and transform abstract ideas into deployable, monetizable assets.

steps.title: Define the Minimum Viable Feature
steps.description: Stop building 'apps' and start building a single, high-value feature that solves a specific pain point. This prevents scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this business idea: [Insert Idea]. Break it down into one single, high-value MVP feature. List the database schema and the core API endpoints required to make this functional.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project README.md documenting the architecture defined by the MVP feature list.'

steps.title: Initialize the Technical Foundation
steps.description: Standardize your repository setup so you aren't wasting time on boilerplate configuration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready folder structure for a SaaS MVP using [Language/Framework]. Include essential configs for environment variables and CI/CD.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Scaffold the project directory structure and initialize the git repository.'

steps.title: Automate Database Schema Logic
steps.description: Let the AI handle the complex relational mappings so you can focus on user experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create the SQL schema for a SaaS user auth and subscription model. Ensure it is optimized for [Database Type].
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate and execute the migration files based on the SQL schema provided.'

steps.title: Build the Core Revenue Loop
steps.description: Focus on the path to payment. If it doesn't lead to a transaction, it isn't a priority.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design the controller logic for a Stripe checkout integration. Define the process from user click to successful payment confirmation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the Stripe webhook handler and order processing service.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Get your code into a live environment immediately. Real user feedback is the only metric that matters.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a deployment script for Vercel/Render that automates the build and environment variable injection for production.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure the deployment pipeline and verify the production environment health checks.'

pro_tips: Never write boilerplate code by hand; if you spend more than 10 minutes on setup, you are doing it wrong.
Treat your GitHub commit history as your project management board; if there are no commits, there is no progress.
Use Claude Code to perform 'self-code reviews' by asking it to find security vulnerabilities in your own generated code.
Limit your MVP development to a strict 14-day cycle; if it takes longer, you are building too much.
Monetization should be the first feature you build, not the last, to ensure the core business logic is sound.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, run a 'Smoke Test' with 5 potential users. Collect feedback, iterate on the AI-generated code, and focus exclusively on improving the conversion rate of your primary revenue hook.
content.conversion_block: The market doesn't care how many tutorials you've watched; it only cares about the value you deliver. Stop waiting for the perfect moment. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
